concerns.text: Task T040 ('Create code/scripts/validate_clinical.py to simulate clinician review') violates Constitution Principle VII. The Constitution mandates 'two board-certified clinicians MUST independently verify a random sample'. A script simulation is a semantic weakening of the requirement for human expert validation and cannot satisfy the 'Clinical Ground-Truth' constraint.

Task T034 ('Implement Kruskal-Wallis test on accuracy drop distributions') contradicts Spec FR-004 and US-3 which explicitly mandate the test be performed on the 'distribution of per-item resilience scores'. Using 'accuracy drop' instead of 'resilience scores' is a semantic relaxation of the statistical requirement.

Task T033 ('Implement Wilcoxon signed-rank test') is present, but the Plan (Phase 3) and Complexity Tracking table incorrectly reference 'McNemar's Test'. While the task text is correct, the surrounding plan context suggests a drift in the statistical methodology that the task must strictly adhere to. Ensure T033 strictly implements Wilcoxon on per-item correctness vectors as per FR-004, not McNemar.
